tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Yang mencabut manusia) yang menghempaskan mereka dari tempat perlindungannya di bawah tanah, kemudian angin itu menjatuhkannya ke kepala mereka sehingga matilah mereka semua dan terpisahlah kepala dari tubuh mereka (seakan-akan mereka) yakni keadaan mereka pada waktu itu sebagaimana yang telah disebutkan tadi (pokok-pokok) atau batang-batang (kurma yang tumbang) maksudnya, keadaan mereka ketika itu bagaikan pokok-pokok kurma yang tumbang ke bumi. Mereka diserupakan dengan pokok kurma karena keadaan tubuh mereka yang tinggi-tinggi. Lafal Munqa'ir disebutkan ayat ini dalam bentuk Mudzakkar, sedangkan di dalam surah Al Haaqqah kata sifatnya disebutkan dalam bentuk Ta`nits yaitu, di dalam firman-Nya, "batang-batang pohon kurma yang telah kosong (lapuk)." (Q.S. Al-Haaqqah, 7) Demikian itu karena demi memelihara kesamaan bunyi pada akhir ayat pada kedua tempat tersebut

ar: فَكَیۡفَ كَانَ عَذَابِی وَنُذُرِ
ar_simple: فَكَيْفَ كَانَ عَذَابِي وَنُذُرِ

t.sahih-int: And how [severe] were My punishment and warning
t.asad: for, how severe is the suffering which I inflict when My warnings are disregarded
t.yusuf-ali: Yea, how (terrible) was My Penalty and My Warning
t.pickthall: Then see how (dreadful) was My punishment after My warnings
t.arberry: How then were My chastisement and My warnings
t.hilali-khan: Then, how (terrible) was My Torment and My Warnings
t.itani: So how were My punishment and My warnings?
t.mubarakpuri: Then, how was My torment and My warnings
t.qarib: How terrible was My punishment and My warning
t.wahiduddin: How [dreadful] was My punishment and My warning
t.shakir: How (great) was then My punishment and My warning
t.haleem: How [terrible] was My punishment and [the fulfilment of] My warnings
t.maariful: So how was My torment and My warnings
t.khattab: Then how ˹dreadful˺ were My punishment and warnings
t.ghali: How then were My torment and My warnings
t.irving: How were My torment, and My warnings
t.iwq: And how severe were My punishment and warning

t_id.kemenag: Maka alangkah dahsyatnya azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku.
t_id.sabiq: Alangkah dahsyatnya azab dan peringatan-Ku bagi orang-orang yang melanggar!
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka betapa dahsyatnya azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku)
t_id.iwq: Maka alangkah dahsyatnya azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فكيف كان عذابي ونذر»
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَكَيۡفَ كَانَ عَذَابِي وَنُذُرِ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Maka betapa dahsyatnya azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku

ar: وَلَقَدۡ یَسَّرۡنَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ لِلذِّكۡرِ فَهَلۡ مِن مُّدَّكِرࣲ
ar_simple: وَلَقَدْ يَسَّرْنَا الْقُرْآنَ لِلذِّكْرِ فَهَلْ مِنْ مُدَّكِرٍ

t.sahih-int: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember
t.asad: Hence, indeed, We made this Qur’an easy to bear in mind: who, then, is willing to take it to heart
t.yusuf-ali: But We have indeed made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember: then is there any that will receive admonition
t.pickthall: And in truth We have made the Qur'an easy to remember; but is there any that remembereth
t.arberry: Now We have made the Koran easy for Remembrance. Is there any that will remember
t.hilali-khan: And We have indeed made the Quran easy to understand and remember, then is there any that will remember (or receive admonition)
t.itani: We made the Quran easy to remember. Is there anyone who would remember?
t.mubarakpuri: And We have indeed made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember; then is there any that will remember
t.qarib: Hence, We made it easy to bear this Qur'an in mind. Who, then, is willing to keep it in mind
t.wahiduddin: We have made it easy to learn lessons from the Quran: is there anyone who would receive admonition
t.shakir: And certainly We have made the Quran easy for remembrance, but is there anyone who will mind
t.haleem: We have made it easy to learn lessons from the Quran: will anyone take heed
t.maariful: Indeed We have made the Qur’ān easy for seeking advice. So, is there one to heed to the advice
t.khattab: And We have certainly made the Quran easy to remember. So is there anyone who will be mindful
t.ghali: And indeed We have already made the Qur'an easy for remembrance. Is there then any that will recollect
t.irving: We have made the Quran easy to memorize; yet will anyone memorize it
t.iwq: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami mudahkan Al Quran untuk pelajaran, maka adakah orang yang mengambil pelajaran?
t_id.sabiq: Kami telah memudahkan al-Qur'ân untuk dijadikan peringatan dan pelajaran. Adakah orang yang mau menjadikannya pelajaran?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya telah kami mudahkan Alquran untuk pelajaran, maka adakah orang yang mengambil pelajaran?)
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami mudahkan Al Al-Quran untuk pelajaran, maka adakah orang yang mengambil pelajaran?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد يسرنا القرآن للذكر فهل من مدكر»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ يَسَّرۡنَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ لِلذِّكۡرِ فَهَلۡ مِن مُّدَّكِرٖ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Dan sesungguhnya telah kami mudahkan Alquran untuk pelajaran, maka adakah orang yang mengambil pelajaran

ar: كَذَّبَتۡ ثَمُودُ بِٱلنُّذُرِ
ar_simple: كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ بِالنُّذُرِ

t.sahih-int: Thamud denied the warning
t.asad: [AND the tribe of] Thamud gave the lie to all [Our] warnings
t.yusuf-ali: The Thamud (also) rejected (their) Warners
t.pickthall: (The tribe of) Thamud rejected warnings
t.arberry: Thamood cried lies to the warnings
t.hilali-khan: Thamud (people also) belied the warnings
t.itani: Thamood rejected the warnings.
t.mubarakpuri: Thamud denied the warnings.
t.qarib: The people of Thamud rejected the warnings
t.wahiduddin: The tribe of Thamud also rejected Our warnings
t.shakir: Samood rejected the warning
t.haleem: The people of Thamud also rejected the warnings
t.maariful: (The people of) Thamūd rejected the warners
t.khattab: Thamûd rejected the warnings ˹as well˺
t.ghali: Thamud cried lies to the warnings
t.irving: Thamud rejected My warnings too
t.iwq: Thamud denied the warning

t_id.kemenag: Kaum Tsamudpun telah mendustakan ancaman-ancaman (itu).
t_id.sabiq: Kaum Tsamûd telah mendustakan peringatan-peringatan Shâlih, seorang nabi yang diutus kepada mereka.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kaum Tsamud pun telah mendustakan ancaman-ancaman itu) lafal An Nudzur adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Nadziirun yang bermakna Mundzirun, yaitu perkara-perkara yang dijadikan peringatan oleh nabi mereka (Nabi Saleh) jika mereka tidak mau beriman dan tidak mau mengikutinya.
t_id.iwq: Kaum Tsamudpun telah mendustakan ancaman-ancaman (itu).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «كذبت ثمود بالنذر» جمع نذير بمعنى منذر، أي بالأمور التي أنذرهم بها نبيهم صالح إن لم يؤمنوا به ويتبعوه
tafsir.siraj-ar: كَذَّبَتۡ ثَمُودُ بِٱلنُّذُرِ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kaum Tsamud pun telah mendustakan ancaman-ancaman itu) lafal An Nudzur adalah bentuk jamak dari lafal Nadziirun yang bermakna Mundzirun, yaitu perkara-perkara yang dijadikan peringatan oleh nabi mereka (Nabi Saleh) jika mereka tidak mau beriman dan tidak mau mengikutinya

ar: فَقَالُوۤا۟ أَبَشَرࣰا مِّنَّا وَ ٰحِدࣰا نَّتَّبِعُهُۥۤ إِنَّاۤ إِذࣰا لَّفِی ضَلَـٰلࣲ وَسُعُرٍ
ar_simple: فَقَالُوا أَبَشَرًا مِنَّا وَاحِدًا نَتَّبِعُهُ إِنَّا إِذًا لَفِي ضَلَالٍ وَسُعُرٍ

t.sahih-int: And said, "Is it one human being among us that we should follow? Indeed, we would then be in error and madness
t.asad: and they said: “Are we to follow one single mortal, one from among ourselves? In that case, behold, we would certainly sink into error and folly
t.yusuf-ali: For they said: "What! a man! a Solitary one from among ourselves! shall we follow such a one? Truly should we then be straying in mind, and mad
t.pickthall: For they said; Is it a mortal man, alone among us, that we are to follow? Then indeed we should fall into error and madness
t.arberry: and said, 'What, shall we follow a mortal, one out of ourselves? Then indeed we should be in error and insanity
t.hilali-khan: For they said: "A man! Alone from among us, that we are to follow? Truly, then we should be in error and distress or madness
t.itani: They said, “Are we to follow one of us, a human being? We would then go astray, and end up in Hell.
t.mubarakpuri: And they said: "A man, alone among us -- shall we follow him Truly, then we should be in error and distress!"
t.qarib: They said, "Are we to follow a human being, one of us? We would then be misguided and quite insane
t.wahiduddin: they said, "Are we to follow a man from amongst ourselves? We would surely then fall into error and madness
t.shakir: So they said: What! a single mortal from among us! Shall we follow him? Most surely we shall in that case be in sure error and distress
t.haleem: they said, ‘What? A man? Why should we follow a lone man from amongst ourselves? That would be misguided; quite insane
t.maariful: So they said, “Shall we follow a single human being from among us? Then we will be in error and insanity
t.khattab: arguing, “How can we follow one ˹average˺ human being from among us? We would then truly be misguided and insane
t.ghali: So they said, "Should we closely follow a mortal, one out of ourselves? Lo, surely we are indeed in error and insanity
t.irving: and they said: "Are we to follow a single human being from among ourselves? We would then be in error and frenzy
t.iwq: And said, "Is it one human being among us that we should follow? Indeed, we would then be in error and madness

t_id.kemenag: Maka mereka berkata: "Bagaimana kita akan mengikuti seorang manusia (biasa) di antara kita?" Sesungguhnya kalau kita begitu benar-benar berada dalam keadaan sesat dan gila".
t_id.sabiq: Mereka berkata, "Apakah seorang manusia biasa di antara kita yang harus kita ikuti? Kalau kita mengikutinya, kita tentu akan tersesat dan gila.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka mereka berkata, "Apakah kepada manusia) lafal Basyaran dinashabkan karena Isytighal (yakni seseorang di antara kami) kedua lafal ini menjadi sifat bagi lafal Basyaran (kami harus mengikutinya) merupakan penafsir dari Fi'il yang menashabkan lafal Basyaran. Istifham atau kata tanya di sini mengandung makna Nafi atau negatif. Maksudnya, bagaimana kami harus mengikutinya sedangkan kami adalah golongan mayoritas dan dia adalah seseorang di antara kami, lagi dia bukanlah seorang raja. Maksud mereka ialah bahwa mereka tidak mau mengikutinya. (Sesungguhnya kami kalau demikian) jika kami mengikutinya (benar-benar berada dalam kesesatan) atau menyimpang dari kebenaran (dan gila) atau tidak berakal waras.
t_id.iwq: Maka mereka berkata: "Bagaimana kita akan mengikuti seorang manusia (biasa) di antara kita?" Sesungguhnya kalau kita begitu benar-benar berada dalam keadaan sesat dan gila".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فقالوا أبشرا» منصوب على الاشتغال «منا واحدا» صفتان لـ بشرا «نتبعه» مفسر للفعل الناصب له والاستفهام بمعنى النفي المعنى كيف نتبعه ونحن جماعة كثيرة وهو واحد منا وليس بملك، أي لا نتبعه «إنا إذًا» إن اتبعناه «لفي ضلال» ذهاب عن الصواب «وسعر» جنون
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَقَالُوٓاْ أَبَشَرٗا مِّنَّا وَٰحِدٗا نَّتَّبِعُهُۥٓ إِنَّآ إِذٗا لَّفِي ضَلَٰلٖ وَسُعُرٍ (وَسُعُرٍ: جُنُونٍ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka mereka berkata, "Apakah kepada manusia) lafal Basyaran dinashabkan karena Isytighal (yakni seseorang di antara kami) kedua lafal ini menjadi sifat bagi lafal Basyaran (kami harus mengikutinya) merupakan penafsir dari Fi'il yang menashabkan lafal Basyaran. Istifham atau kata tanya di sini mengandung makna Nafi atau negatif. Maksudnya, bagaimana kami harus mengikutinya sedangkan kami adalah golongan mayoritas dan dia adalah seseorang di antara kami, lagi dia bukanlah seorang raja. Maksud mereka ialah bahwa mereka tidak mau mengikutinya. (Sesungguhnya kami kalau demikian) jika kami mengikutinya (benar-benar berada dalam kesesatan) atau menyimpang dari kebenaran (dan gila) atau tidak berakal waras

ar: أَءُلۡقِیَ ٱلذِّكۡرُ عَلَیۡهِ مِنۢ بَیۡنِنَا بَلۡ هُوَ كَذَّابٌ أَشِرࣱ
ar_simple: أَأُلْقِيَ الذِّكْرُ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ بَيْنِنَا بَلْ هُوَ كَذَّابٌ أَشِرٌ

t.sahih-int: Has the message been sent down upon him from among us? Rather, he is an insolent liar
t.asad: Why - on him alone from among all of us should a [divine] reminder have been bestowed? Nay, but he is a boastful liar!”
t.yusuf-ali: Is it that the Message is sent to him, of all people amongst us? Nay, he is a liar, an insolent one
t.pickthall: Hath the remembrance been given unto him alone among us? Nay, but he is a rash liar
t.arberry: Has the Reminder been cast upon him alone among us? Nay, rather he is an impudent liar
t.hilali-khan: Is it that the Reminder is sent to him [Prophet Salih (Saleh)] alone from among us? Nay, he is an insolent liar
t.itani: Was the message given to him, out of all of us? In fact, he is a wicked liar.”
t.mubarakpuri: "Is it that the Reminder is sent to him alone from among us Nay, he is an insolent liar!"
t.qarib: Why should he alone from among all of us be chosen to receive the message? He is nothing but a wicked liar
t.wahiduddin: Has the [Divine] message been revealed to him alone of all of us? No, he is a boastful liar
t.shakir: Has the reminder been made to light upon him from among us? Nay! he is an insolent liar
t.haleem: Would a mes-sage be given to him alone out of all of us? No, he is an insolent liar!’
t.maariful: Is it that the advice has been cast upon him alone out of all of us? No, but he is a bragging liar.”
t.khattab: Has the revelation been sent down ˹only˺ to him out of ˹all of˺ us? In fact, he is a boastful liar.”
t.ghali: Has the Remembrance been cast upon him (alone) from among us? No indeed, (but) he is an insolent liar
t.irving: Has the Reminder been delivered merely to him among Us? Instead he is a brash liar
t.iwq: Has the message been sent down upon him from among us? Rather, he is an insolent liar

t_id.kemenag: Apakah wahyu itu diturunkan kepadanya di antara kita? Sebenarnya dia adalah seorang yang amat pendusta lagi sombong.
t_id.sabiq: Apakah wahyu itu diturunkan kepadanya, sementara di antara kita ada yang lebih pantas daripada dirinya? Sebenarnya ia hanyalah seorang yang banyak berbohong dan mengingkari nikmat."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Apakah diturunkan) boleh dibaca Tahqiq dan boleh pula dibaca Tas-hil (wahyu itu) peringatan itu (kepadanya di antara kita) maksudnya, tidak pantas wahyu diturunkan kepadanya. (Sebenarnya dia adalah seorang yang amat pendusta) di dalam pengakuannya itu yang menyatakan bahwa hal tersebut telah diwahyukan kepadanya (lagi sombong") congkak dan takabur. Lalu Allah berfirman,
t_id.iwq: Apakah wahyu itu diturunkan kepadanya di antara kita? Sebenarnya dia adalah seorang yang amat pendusta lagi sombong.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أألقي» بتحقيق الهمزتين وتسهيل الثانية وإدخال ألف بينهما على الوجهين وتركه «الذكر» الوحي «عليه من بيننا» أي لم يوح إليه «بل هو كذاب» في قوله إنه أوحي إليه ما ذكر «أشر» متكبر بطر، قال تعالى
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَءُلۡقِيَ ٱلذِّكۡرُ عَلَيۡهِ مِنۢ بَيۡنِنَا بَلۡ هُوَ كَذَّابٌ أَشِرٞ (أَشِرٌ: مُتَكَبِّرٌ، مُتَجَبِّرٌ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Apakah diturunkan) boleh dibaca Tahqiq dan boleh pula dibaca Tas-hil (wahyu itu) peringatan itu (kepadanya di antara kita) maksudnya, tidak pantas wahyu diturunkan kepadanya. (Sebenarnya dia adalah seorang yang amat pendusta) di dalam pengakuannya itu yang menyatakan bahwa hal tersebut telah diwahyukan kepadanya (lagi sombong") congkak dan takabur. Lalu Allah berfirman

ar: سَیَعۡلَمُونَ غَدࣰا مَّنِ ٱلۡكَذَّابُ ٱلۡأَشِرُ
ar_simple: سَيَعْلَمُونَ غَدًا مَنِ الْكَذَّابُ الْأَشِرُ

t.sahih-int: They will know tomorrow who is the insolent liar
t.asad: [And God said:] “On the morrow they will come to know who the boastful liar is
t.yusuf-ali: Ah! they will know on the morrow, which is the liar, the insolent one
t.pickthall: (Unto their warner it was said): To-morrow they will know who is the rash liar
t.arberry: They shall surely know tomorrow who is the impudent liar
t.hilali-khan: Tomorrow they will come to know, who is the liar, the insolent one
t.itani: They will know tomorrow who the wicked liar is.
t.mubarakpuri: Tomorrow they will come to know who is the liar, the insolent one!
t.qarib: They will know tomorrow who the wicked liar is
t.wahiduddin: [We said to him] "Tomorrow they shall know who is the boastful liar
t.shakir: Tomorrow shall they know who is the liar, the insolent one
t.haleem: ‘Tomorrow they will know who is the insolent liar
t.maariful: Tomorrow they will know who the bragging liar is
t.khattab: ˹It was revealed to Ṣâliḥ,˺ “They will soon know who the boastful liar is
t.ghali: They will (soon) know tomorrow who is the insolent liar
t.irving: Tomorrow they will know who the brash liar is
t.iwq: And they will know tomorrow who is the insolent liar

t_id.kemenag: Kelak mereka akan mengetahui siapakah yang sebenarnya amat pendusta lagi sombong.
t_id.sabiq: Kelak, pada hari diturunkannya azab, mereka akan mengetahui siapa sebenarnya yang berdusta dan mengingkari nikmat: merekakah atau Shâlih, rasul mereka?
t_id.jalalayn-id: ("Kelak mereka akan mengetahui) di akhirat nanti (siapakah yang sebenarnya amat pendusta lagi sombong") dia ataukah mereka? Kelak Allah pasti akan mengazab mereka yang telah mendustakan nabinya yakni, Nabi Saleh.
t_id.iwq: Kelak mereka akan mengetahui siapakah yang sebenarnya amat pendusta lagi sombong.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «سيعلمون غدا» في الآخرة «من الكذاب الأشر» وهو أو هم بأن يعذبوا على تكذيبهم نبيهم صالحا
tafsir.siraj-ar: سَيَعۡلَمُونَ غَدٗا مَّنِ ٱلۡكَذَّابُ ٱلۡأَشِرُ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kelak mereka akan mengetahui) di akhirat nanti (siapakah yang sebenarnya amat pendusta lagi sombong") dia ataukah mereka? Kelak Allah pasti akan mengazab mereka yang telah mendustakan nabinya yakni, Nabi Saleh

ar: إِنَّا مُرۡسِلُوا۟ ٱلنَّاقَةِ فِتۡنَةࣰ لَّهُمۡ فَٱرۡتَقِبۡهُمۡ وَٱصۡطَبِرۡ
ar_simple: إِنَّا مُرْسِلُو النَّاقَةِ فِتْنَةً لَهُمْ فَارْتَقِبْهُمْ وَاصْطَبِرْ

t.sahih-int: Indeed, We are sending the she-camel as trial for them, so watch them and be patient
t.asad: Behold, [O Salih] We are letting loose this she-camel as a test for them; and thou but watch them, and contain thyself in patience
t.yusuf-ali: For We will send the she-camel by way of trial for them. So watch them, (O Salih), and possess thyself in patience
t.pickthall: Lo! We are sending the she-camel as a test for them; so watch them and have patience
t.arberry: They shall send the She-camel as a trial for them; so watch thou them and keep patience
t.hilali-khan: Verily, We are sending the she-camel as a test for them. So watch them [O Salih (Saleh)], and be patient
t.itani: We are sending the she-camel as a test for them; so watch them and be patient.
t.mubarakpuri: Verily, We are sending the she-camel as a test for them. So watch them, and be patient!
t.qarib: We are sending the she-camel as a test for them. Just watch them and be patient
t.wahiduddin: for We are [going to] send the she-camel as a trial for them, so watch them and be patient
t.shakir: Surely We are going to send the she-camel as a trial for them; therefore watch them and have patience
t.haleem: for We shall send them a she-camel to test them: so watch them [Salih] and be patient
t.maariful: We are going to send the she-camel as a trial for them; so watch them (O SāliH,) and keep patience
t.khattab: We are sending the she-camel as a test for them. So watch them ˹closely˺, and have patience
t.ghali: Surely We are sending the she-camel as a temptation for them; so be on the watch for them and constantly (endure) patiently
t.irving: We are sending them a she-camel as a test, so watch them and act patient
t.iwq: Indeed, We are sending the she-camel as trial for them, so watch them and be patient

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya Kami akan mengirimkan unta betina sebagai cobaan bagi mereka, maka tunggulah (tindakan) mereka dan bersabarlah.
t_id.sabiq: Kami, sungguh, akan mengirim unta betina--sebagai mukjizat Shâlih, rasul Kami--untuk menguji mereka. Nantikan dan lihatlah tindakan mereka. Bersabarlah dalam menghadapi tindakan mereka yang menyakitkan sampai keputusan Allah tiba.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Kami akan mengirimkan unta betina) yaitu mengeluarkan unta dari batu besar yang terdapat di lembah tempat mereka sebagaimana yang mereka minta (sebagai cobaan) ujian (bagi mereka) Kami lakukan hal itu guna mencoba mereka (maka tunggulah mereka) hai Saleh apa yang akan mereka kerjakan dan apa yang akan ditimpakan kepada mereka (dan bersabarlah) atas perlakuan mereka yang menyakitkan itu terhadap dirimu. Lafal Ishthabir pada asalnya adalah Ishtabir, kemudian huruf Ta diganti menjadi huruf Tha sehingga jadilah Ishthabir, maknanya bersabarlah kamu.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya Kami akan mengirimkan unta betina sebagai cobaan bagi mereka, maka tunggulah (tindakan) mereka dan bersabarlah.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إنا مرسلو الناقة» مخرجوها من الهضبة الصخرة كما سألوا «فتنة» محنة «لهم» لنخبرهم «فارتقبهم» يا صالح أي انتظر ما هم صانعون وما يصنع بهم «واصطبر» الطاء بدل من تاء الافتعال أي اصبر على أذاهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّا مُرۡسِلُواْ ٱلنَّاقَةِ فِتۡنَةٗ لَّهُمۡ فَٱرۡتَقِبۡهُمۡ وَٱصۡطَبِرۡ (فِتْنَةً لَّهُمْ: اخْتِبَارًا لَهُمْ, فَارْتَقِبْهُمْ: انْتَظِرْ يَا صَالِحُ مَا يَحُلُّ بِهِمْ مِنَ العَذَابِ, وَاصْطَبِرْ: اصْبِرْ عَلَى الدَّعْوَةِ، وَالأَذَى)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Kami akan mengirimkan unta betina) yaitu mengeluarkan unta dari batu besar yang terdapat di lembah tempat mereka sebagaimana yang mereka minta (sebagai cobaan) ujian (bagi mereka) Kami lakukan hal itu guna mencoba mereka (maka tunggulah mereka) hai Saleh apa yang akan mereka kerjakan dan apa yang akan ditimpakan kepada mereka (dan bersabarlah) atas perlakuan mereka yang menyakitkan itu terhadap dirimu. Lafal Ishthabir pada asalnya adalah Ishtabir, kemudian huruf Ta diganti menjadi huruf Tha sehingga jadilah Ishthabir, maknanya bersabarlah kamu

ar: وَنَبِّئۡهُمۡ أَنَّ ٱلۡمَاۤءَ قِسۡمَةُۢ بَیۡنَهُمۡۖ كُلُّ شِرۡبࣲ مُّحۡتَضَرࣱ
ar_simple: وَنَبِّئْهُمْ أَنَّ الْمَاءَ قِسْمَةٌ بَيْنَهُمْ ۖ كُلُّ شِرْبٍ مُحْتَضَرٌ

t.sahih-int: And inform them that the water is shared between them, each [day of] drink attended [by turn]
t.asad: And let them know that the water [of their wells] is to be divided between them, with each share of water equitably apportioned.”
t.yusuf-ali: And tell them that the water is to be divided between them: Each one's right to drink being brought forward (by suitable turns)
t.pickthall: And inform them that the water is to be shared between (her and) them. Every drinking will be witnessed
t.arberry: And tell them that the water is to be divided between them, each drink for each in turn
t.hilali-khan: And inform them that the water is to be shared between (her and) them. Each one's right to drink being established (by turns)
t.itani: And inform them that the water is to be shared between them; each share of drink made available.
t.mubarakpuri: And inform them that the water is to be shared between (her and) them, each one's right to drink being established (by turns).
t.qarib: Tell them that the water is to be shared between them, with each share equitably apportioned
t.wahiduddin: And tell them that the water [of the well] is to be divided between them, and that each one should drink in turn
t.shakir: And inform them that the water is shared between them; every share of the water shall be regulated
t.haleem: Tell them the water is to be shared between them: each one should drink in turn.’
t.maariful: and tell them that water (of the well) is to be shared between them (and the she-camel), so as the right of having water shall be attended by each (alternatively)
t.khattab: And tell them that the ˹drinking˺ water must be divided between them ˹and her˺, each taking a turn to drink ˹every other day˺.”
t.ghali: And fully inform them that the water is to a (fair) division between them; (i.e., between the she-camel and them) each drinking is for each presented in turn
t.irving: Announce to them how water must be shared among them; each will have his own special time to drink
t.iwq: And inform them that the water is shared between them, each day of drink attended by turn

t_id.kemenag: Dan beritakanlah kepada mereka bahwa sesungguhnya air itu terbagi antara mereka (dengan unta betina itu); tiap-tiap giliran minum dihadiri (oleh yang punya giliran)
t_id.sabiq: Beritahukan mereka bahwa air itu milik bersama antara mereka dan unta betina itu. Masing-masing mendapat giliran minum pada hari yang ditentukan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan beritakanlah kepada mereka bahwa sesungguhnya air itu terbagi) dibagi-bagi (antara mereka) dengan unta betina itu; yakni sehari buat mereka dan hari yang lainnya buat unta betina, demikianlah seterusnya (tiap-tiap minum) bagian air (dihadiri) oleh yang punya giliran, yakni kaum Nabi Saleh dan unta betina. Akhirnya lama kelamaan mereka merasa bosan dengan adanya pembagian ini, lalu mereka bersepakat untuk membunuh unta betina itu.
t_id.iwq: Dan beritakanlah kepada mereka bahwa sesungguhnya air itu terbagi antara mereka (dengan unta betina itu); tiap-tiap giliran minum dihadiri (oleh yang punya giliran)

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ونبئهم أن الماء قسمة» مقسوم «بينهم» وبين الناقة يوم لهم ويوم لها «كلُّ شرب» نصيب من الماء «محتضر» يحضره القوم يومهم والناقة يومها فتمادوا على ذلك ثم ملوه فهموا بقتل الناقة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَنَبِّئۡهُمۡ أَنَّ ٱلۡمَآءَ قِسۡمَةُۢ بَيۡنَهُمۡۖ كُلُّ شِرۡبٖ مُّحۡتَضَرٞ (وَنَبِّئْهُمْ: أَخْبِرْهُمْ, قِسْمَةٌ بَيْنَهُمْ: مَقْسُومٌ بَيْنَ قَوْمِكَ وَالنَّاقَةِ؛ يَوْمٌ لَهُمْ، وَيَوْمٌ لِلنَّاقَةِ, شِرْبٍ: نَصِيبٍ مِنَ المَاءِ, مُّحْتَضَرٌ: يَحْضُرُهُ صَاحِبُهُ فِي يَوْمِهِ، وَيُحْرَمُ مِنْهُ الآخَرُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan beritakanlah kepada mereka bahwa sesungguhnya air itu terbagi) dibagi-bagi (antara mereka) dengan unta betina itu; yakni sehari buat mereka dan hari yang lainnya buat unta betina, demikianlah seterusnya (tiap-tiap minum) bagian air (dihadiri) oleh yang punya giliran, yakni kaum Nabi Saleh dan unta betina. Akhirnya lama kelamaan mereka merasa bosan dengan adanya pembagian ini, lalu mereka bersepakat untuk membunuh unta betina itu

ar: فَنَادَوۡا۟ صَاحِبَهُمۡ فَتَعَاطَىٰ فَعَقَرَ
ar_simple: فَنَادَوْا صَاحِبَهُمْ فَتَعَاطَىٰ فَعَقَرَ

t.sahih-int: But they called their companion, and he dared and hamstrung [her]
t.asad: But they summoned their [boldest] companion, and he ventured [upon the evil deed], and cruelly slaughtered [the animal]
t.yusuf-ali: But they called to their companion, and he took a sword in hand, and hamstrung (her)
t.pickthall: But they call their comrade and he took and hamstrung (her)
t.arberry: Then they called their comrade, and he took in hand, and hamstrung her
t.hilali-khan: But they called their comrade and he took (a sword) and killed (her)
t.itani: But they called their friend, and he dared, and he slaughtered.
t.mubarakpuri: But they called their comrade and he took (a sword) and killed (her).
t.qarib: But they called their friend, who dared to slaughter [her]
t.wahiduddin: But they called their companion who took a sword and hamstrung her
t.shakir: But they called their companion, so he took (the sword) and slew (her)
t.haleem: But they called their companion, who took a sword and hamstrung the camel
t.maariful: Then they called their man (to kill the she-camel,) so he undertook (the task) and killed (the she-camel)
t.khattab: But they roused a companion of theirs, so he dared to kill ˹her˺
t.ghali: Then they called out their companion, so he took upon himself (the task); then he hamstrung her
t.irving: They called out to their companion; and he assumed the task and hamstrung [her]
t.iwq: But they called their companion, and he dared and hamstrung her

t_id.kemenag: Maka mereka memanggil kawannya, lalu kawannya menangkap (unta itu) dan membunuhnya.
t_id.sabiq: Mereka memanggil kawannya yang kemudian bersiap-siap menangkap dan menyembelih unta itu. Alangkah dahsyatnya siksa dan peringatan-Ku bagi orang-orang yang melanggar!
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka mereka memanggil kawannya) yaitu seorang yang gagah perkasa untuk membunuh unta betina itu (lalu kawannya itu menangkap) unta itu seraya menghunus pedangnya (dan menyembelihnya) menyembelih unta betina itu dengan pedangnya dan membunuhnya sesuai dengan apa yang diinginkan oleh mereka.
t_id.iwq: Maka mereka memanggil kawannya, lalu kawannya menangkap (unta itu) dan membunuhnya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فنادوا صاحبهم» قدرا ليقتلها «فتعاطى» تناول السيف «فعقر» به الناقة، أي قتلها موافقة لهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَنَادَوۡاْ صَاحِبَهُمۡ فَتَعَاطَىٰ فَعَقَرَ (فَنَادَوْا صَاحِبَهُمْ: دَعَوْهُ لِيَعْقِرَ النَّاقَةَ, فَتَعَاطَى: تَنَاوَلَ النَّاقَةَ بِيَدِهِ, فَعَقَرَ: نَحَرَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka mereka memanggil kawannya) yaitu seorang yang gagah perkasa untuk membunuh unta betina itu (lalu kawannya itu menangkap) unta itu seraya menghunus pedangnya (dan menyembelihnya) menyembelih unta betina itu dengan pedangnya dan membunuhnya sesuai dengan apa yang diinginkan oleh mereka

ar: فَكَیۡفَ كَانَ عَذَابِی وَنُذُرِ
ar_simple: فَكَيْفَ كَانَ عَذَابِي وَنُذُرِ

t.sahih-int: And how [severe] were My punishment and warning
t.asad: and how severe was the suffering which I inflicted when My warnings were disregarded
t.yusuf-ali: Ah! how (terrible) was My Penalty and My Warning
t.pickthall: Then see how (dreadful) was My punishment after My warnings
t.arberry: How then were My chastisement and My warnings
t.hilali-khan: Then, how (terrible) was My Torment and My Warnings
t.itani: So how were My punishment and My warnings?
t.mubarakpuri: Then, how was My torment and My warnings
t.qarib: How then was My punishment and My warning
t.wahiduddin: How [terrible] were My punishment and My warnings
t.shakir: How (great) was then My punishment and My warning
t.haleem: How [terrible] was My punishment and [the fulfilment of] My warnings
t.maariful: So how was My torment and My warnings
t.khattab: Then how ˹dreadful˺ were My punishment and warnings
t.ghali: How then were My torment and My warnings
t.irving: How were My torment, and My warnings
t.iwq: And how severe were My punishment and warning

t_id.kemenag: Alangkah dahsyatnya azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku.
t_id.sabiq: Mereka memanggil kawannya yang kemudian bersiap-siap menangkap dan menyembelih unta itu. Alangkah dahsyatnya siksa dan peringatan-Ku bagi orang-orang yang melanggar!
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka alangkah dahsyatnya azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku) yakni peringatan-Ku terhadap mereka sebelum azab diturunkan kepada mereka. Lalu Allah menjelaskan hal ini melalui firman selanjutnya,
t_id.iwq: Alangkah dahsyatnya azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فكيف كان عذابي ونذر» إنذاري لهم بالعذاب قبل نزوله، أي وقع موقعه وبيَّنه بقوله
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَكَيۡفَ كَانَ عَذَابِي وَنُذُرِ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka alangkah dahsyatnya azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku) yakni peringatan-Ku terhadap mereka sebelum azab diturunkan kepada mereka. Lalu Allah menjelaskan hal ini melalui firman selanjutnya

ar: إِنَّاۤ أَرۡسَلۡنَا عَلَیۡهِمۡ صَیۡحَةࣰ وَ ٰحِدَةࣰ فَكَانُوا۟ كَهَشِیمِ ٱلۡمُحۡتَظِرِ
ar_simple: إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ صَيْحَةً وَاحِدَةً فَكَانُوا كَهَشِيمِ الْمُحْتَظِرِ

t.sahih-int: Indeed, We sent upon them one blast from the sky, and they became like the dry twig fragments of an [animal] pen
t.asad: Behold, We let loose upon them one single blast [of Our punishment], and they became like the dried-up, crumbling twigs of a sheepfold
t.yusuf-ali: For We sent against them a single Mighty Blast, and they became like the dry stubble used by one who pens cattle
t.pickthall: Lo! We sent upon them one Shout, and they became as the dry twigs (rejected by) the builder of a cattle-fold
t.arberry: We loosed against them one Cry, and they were as the wattles of a pen-builder
t.hilali-khan: Verily, We sent against them a single Saihah (torment - awful cry, etc.), and they became like the dry stubble of a fold-builder
t.itani: We sent against them a single Scream, and they became like crushed hay.
t.mubarakpuri: Verily, We sent against them a single Sayhah, and they became like straw Al-Muhtazir.
t.qarib: We released against them a single blast, and they became like crumbling twigs
t.wahiduddin: Then We sent a single blast against them and they became like dry stubble which has been trampled upon
t.shakir: Surely We sent upon them a single cry, so they were like the dry fragments of trees which the maker of an enclosure collects
t.haleem: We released a single mighty blast against them and they ended up like a fencemaker’s dry sticks
t.maariful: We sent upon them a single Cry, and they were like crushed leafs of a hedge-builder
t.khattab: Indeed, We sent against them ˹only˺ one ˹mighty˺ blast, leaving them like the twigs of fence-builders
t.ghali: Surely We sent against them one Shout; so they were as the chaff of a pen-builder
t.irving: We sent a single Blast against them so they [lay] like dry stalks caught in a fence corner
t.iwq: Indeed, We sent upon them one blast from the sky, and they became like the dry twig fragments of an animal pen

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya Kami menimpakan atas mereka satu suara yang keras mengguntur, maka jadilah mereka seperti rumput kering (yang dikumpulkan oleh) yang punya kandang binatang.
t_id.sabiq: Kami, sungguh, menimpakan kepada mereka satu bunyi yang amat keras. Seketika, mereka menjadi seperti pohon kering yang dikumpulkan oleh orang yang punya kandang.
t_id.jalalayn-id: ("Sesungguhnya Kami menimpakan atas mereka satu suara yang keras mengguntur, maka jadilah mereka seperti kayu-kayu kering yang dijadikan sebagai kandang kambing") Al Muhtazhar artinya pohon dan duri-duri yang kering kemudian dijadikan sebagai kandang kambing untuk menjaga kambing-kambing dari sergapan binatang buas seperti serigala dan binatang pemangsa lainnya. Dan kayu-kayu atau duri-duri kering yang terjatuh dari kandang tersebut lalu diinjak-injak oleh kambing dinamakan Al Hasyiim.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya Kami menimpakan atas mereka satu suara yang keras mengguntur, maka jadilah mereka seperti rumput kering (yang dikumpulkan oleh) yang punya kandang binatang.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إنا أرسلنا عليهم صيحة واحدة فكانوا كهشيم المحتظر» هو الذي يجعل لغنمه حظيرة من يابس الشجر والشوك يحفظهن فيها من الذئاب والسباع وما سقط من ذلك فداسته هو الهشيم
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّآ أَرۡسَلۡنَا عَلَيۡهِمۡ صَيۡحَةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗ فَكَانُواْ كَهَشِيمِ ٱلۡمُحۡتَظِرِ (كَهَشِيمِ الْمُحْتَظِرِ: كَالزَّرْعِ اليَابِسِ الَّذِي دَاسَتْهُ البَهَائِمُ فَتَهَشَّمَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Kami menimpakan atas mereka satu suara yang keras mengguntur, maka jadilah mereka seperti kayu-kayu kering yang dijadikan sebagai kandang kambing") Al Muhtazhar artinya pohon dan duri-duri yang kering kemudian dijadikan sebagai kandang kambing untuk menjaga kambing-kambing dari sergapan binatang buas seperti serigala dan binatang pemangsa lainnya. Dan kayu-kayu atau duri-duri kering yang terjatuh dari kandang tersebut lalu diinjak-injak oleh kambing dinamakan Al Hasyiim

ar: وَلَقَدۡ یَسَّرۡنَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ لِلذِّكۡرِ فَهَلۡ مِن مُّدَّكِرࣲ
ar_simple: وَلَقَدْ يَسَّرْنَا الْقُرْآنَ لِلذِّكْرِ فَهَلْ مِنْ مُدَّكِرٍ

t.sahih-int: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember
t.asad: Hence, indeed, We made this Qur’an easy to bear in mind: who, then, is willing to take it to heart
t.yusuf-ali: And We have indeed made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember: then is there any that will receive admonition
t.pickthall: And in truth We have made the Qur'an easy to remember; but is there any that remembereth
t.arberry: Now We have made the Koran easy for Remembrance. Is there any that will remember
t.hilali-khan: And indeed, We have made the Quran easy to understand and remember, then is there any that will remember (or receive admonition)
t.itani: We made the Quran easy to understand. Is there anyone who would understand?
t.mubarakpuri: And indeed, We have made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember; then is there any that will remember
t.qarib: Hence, We made it easy to bear this Qur'an in mind. Who, then, is willing to keep it in mind
t.wahiduddin: Indeed, We have made the Quran easy to learn lessons from. Is there anyone who would receive admonition
t.shakir: And certainly We have made the Quran easy for remembrance, but is there anyone who will mind
t.haleem: We have made it easy to learn lessons from the Quran: will anyone take heed
t.maariful: Indeed We have made the Qur’ān easy for seeking advice. So, is there one to heed to the advice
t.khattab: And We have certainly made the Quran easy to remember. So is there anyone who will be mindful
t.ghali: And indeed We have already made the Qur'an easy for remembrance. Is there then any that will recollect
t.irving: We have made the Quran easy to memorize; yet will anyone [bother to] memorize it
t.iwq: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami mudahkan Al Quran untuk pelajaran, maka adakah orang yang mengambil pelajaran?
t_id.sabiq: Kami telah memudahkan al-Qur'ân untuk dijadikan sebagai nasihat dan pelajaran. Adakah yang mau menjadikannya pelajaran?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami mudahkan Alquran untuk pelajaran, maka adakah orang yang mau mengambil pelajaran?)
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami mudahkan Al Al-Quran untuk pelajaran, maka adakah orang yang mengambil pelajaran?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد يسرنا القرآن للذكر فهل من مدكر»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ يَسَّرۡنَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ لِلذِّكۡرِ فَهَلۡ مِن مُّدَّكِرٖ (مُّدَّكِرٍ: مُتَّعِظٍ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami mudahkan Alquran untuk pelajaran, maka adakah orang yang mau mengambil pelajaran

ar: كَذَّبَتۡ قَوۡمُ لُوطِۭ بِٱلنُّذُرِ
ar_simple: كَذَّبَتْ قَوْمُ لُوطٍ بِالنُّذُرِ

t.sahih-int: The people of Lot denied the warning
t.asad: LOT ’S PEOPLE [too] gave the lie to all [Our] warnings
t.yusuf-ali: The people of Lut rejected (his) warning
t.pickthall: The folk of Lot rejected warnings
t.arberry: The people of Lot cried lies to the warnings
t.hilali-khan: The people of Lout (Lot) belied the warnings
t.itani: The people of Lot rejected the warnings.
t.mubarakpuri: The people of Lut denied the warnings.
t.qarib: The people of Lot rejected the warnings
t.wahiduddin: The people of Lot rejected Our warnings
t.shakir: The people of Lut treated the warning. as a lie
t.haleem: The people of Lot rejected the warnings
t.maariful: The people of LūT rejected the warners
t.khattab: The people of Lot ˹also˺ rejected the warnings
t.ghali: The people of Lut (Lot) cried lies to the warnings
t.irving: Lot´s people rejected the warnings
t.iwq: The people of Lot denied the warning

t_id.kemenag: Kaum Luth-pun telah mendustakan ancaman-ancaman (nabinya).
t_id.sabiq: Kaum Lûth telah mendustakan peringatan-peringatan rasulnya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kaum Luth pun telah mendustakan ancaman-ancaman itu) mendustakan hal-hal yang diancamkan kepada mereka melalui lisan Nabi Luth.
t_id.iwq: Kaum Luth-pun telah mendustakan ancaman-ancaman (nabinya).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «كذبت قوم لوط بالنذر» بالأمور المنذرة لهم على لسانه
tafsir.siraj-ar: كَذَّبَتۡ قَوۡمُ لُوطِۭ بِٱلنُّذُرِ (بِالنُّذُرِ: بِآيَاتِ اللهِ الَّتِي أُنْذِرُوا بِهَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kaum Luth pun telah mendustakan ancaman-ancaman itu) mendustakan hal-hal yang diancamkan kepada mereka melalui lisan Nabi Luth

ar: إِنَّاۤ أَرۡسَلۡنَا عَلَیۡهِمۡ حَاصِبًا إِلَّاۤ ءَالَ لُوطࣲۖ نَّجَّیۡنَـٰهُم بِسَحَرࣲ
ar_simple: إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ حَاصِبًا إِلَّا آلَ لُوطٍ ۖ نَجَّيْنَاهُمْ بِسَحَرٍ

t.sahih-int: Indeed, We sent upon them a storm of stones, except the family of Lot - We saved them before dawn
t.asad: [and so,] behold, We let loose upon them a deadly tempest; and only Lot ’s kinsfolk did We save at the break of dawn
t.yusuf-ali: We sent against them a violent Tornado with showers of stones, (which destroyed them), except Lut's household: them We delivered by early Dawn
t.pickthall: Lo! We sent a storm of stones upon them (all) save the family of Lot, whom We rescued in the last watch of the night
t.arberry: We loosed against them a squall of pebbles except the folk of Lot; We delivered them at the dawn
t.hilali-khan: Verily, We sent against them a violent storm of stones (which destroyed them all), except the family of Lout (Lot), whom We saved in last hour of the night
t.itani: We unleashed upon them a shower of stones, except for the family of Lot; We rescued them at dawn.
t.mubarakpuri: Verily, We sent against them Hasib (a violent storm of stones), except the family of Lut, them We saved in the last hour of the night,
t.qarib: We unleashed upon them a shower of stones, except for the family of Lot; We rescued them before dawn
t.wahiduddin: We sent a sandstorm against them which destroyed them all, except the family of Lot, whom We saved at the break of dawn
t.shakir: Surely We sent upon them a stonestorm, except Lut's followers; We saved them a little before daybreak
t.haleem: We released a stonebearing wind against them, all except the family of Lot. We saved them before dawn
t.maariful: We sent upon them a rain of stones, except the family of LūT whom We saved in the last hours of night
t.khattab: We unleashed upon them a storm of stones. As for ˹the believers of˺ Lot’s family, We delivered them before dawn
t.ghali: Surely We sent against them a (squall of) gravel, except the house of Lut; (LOt) We safely delivered them before dawn
t.irving: We sent a sandstorm against them, except for Lot´s own household whom We saved at daybreak
t.iwq: Indeed, We sent upon them a storm of stones, except the family of Lot - We saved them before dawn

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya Kami telah menghembuskan kepada mereka angin yang membawa batu-batu (yang menimpa mereka), kecuali keluarga Luth. Mereka Kami selamatkan sebelum fajar menyingsing,
t_id.sabiq: Kami telah meniupkan angin keras yang melemparkan bebatuan kepada mereka, kecuali kaum Nabi Lûth yang beriman yang Kami selamatkan dari azab ini saat menjelang fajar, sebagai sebuah pemberian nikmat Kami untuk mereka. Dengan nikmat yang besar seperti itu, Kami memberikan balasan kepada orang yang mensyukuri nikmat Kami dengan cara beriman dan patuh.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Kami telah mengembuskan kepada mereka angin yang membawa terbang batu-batu) yakni angin yang melempari mereka dengan batu-batu yang diterbangkannya. Al Hashbaa adalah batu-batu kecil yang besarnya lebih kecil dari genggaman tangan; akhirnya binasalah mereka karenanya (kecuali keluarga Luth) mereka adalah Nabi Luth dan kedua orang putrinya. (Mereka Kami selamatkan di waktu sebelum fajar menyingsing) berasal dari lafal As-haar artinya, waktu menjelang subuh dari hari yang tidak ditentukan. Dan seandainya hari yang dimaksud adalah hari yang ditentukan, niscaya ungkapannya tidak memakai harakat Tanwin karena termasuk Isim yang Ma'rifat dan dima'dul dari lafal As Sahar. Karena, bila dimaksud sebagai hari yang ditentukan pasti memakai Alif dan Lam. Apakah batu kerikil itu dihembuskan kepada keluarga Nabi Luth atau tidak? Sehubungan dengan hal ini ada dua pendapat. Menurut pendapat pertama yakni dihembuskan juga, berarti Istisna di sini bersifat Muttashil. Menurut pendapat kedua yakni, tidak dihembuskan berarti Istitsnanya bersifat Munqathi'. Akan tetapi pada akhirnya keluarga Nabi Luth diselamatkan dari azab itu.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya Kami telah menghembuskan kepada mereka angin yang membawa batu-batu (yang menimpa mereka), kecuali keluarga Luth. Mereka Kami selamatkan sebelum fajar menyingsing,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إنا أرسلنا عليهم حاصبا» ريحا ترميهم بالحصباء وهي صغار الحجارة الواحد دون ملء الكف فهلكوا «إلا آل لوط» وهم ابنتاه معه «نجيناهم بسحر» من الأسحار وقت الصبح من يوم غير معين ولو أريد من يوم معين لمنع من الصرف لأنه معرفة معدول عن السحر لأن حقه أن يستعمل في المعرفة بأل وهل أرسل الحاصب على آل لوط أولا؟ قولان وعبر عن الاستثناء على الأول بأنه متصل وعلى الثاني بأنه منقطع وإن كان من الجنس تسمحا
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّآ أَرۡسَلۡنَا عَلَيۡهِمۡ حَاصِبًا إِلَّآ ءَالَ لُوطٖۖ نَّجَّيۡنَٰهُم بِسَحَرٖ (حَاصِبًا: حِجَارَةً, بِسَحَرٍ: فِي آخِرِ اللَّيْلِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Kami telah mengembuskan kepada mereka angin yang membawa terbang batu-batu) yakni angin yang melempari mereka dengan batu-batu yang diterbangkannya. Al Hashbaa adalah batu-batu kecil yang besarnya lebih kecil dari genggaman tangan; akhirnya binasalah mereka karenanya (kecuali keluarga Luth) mereka adalah Nabi Luth dan kedua orang putrinya. (Mereka Kami selamatkan di waktu sebelum fajar menyingsing) berasal dari lafal As-haar artinya, waktu menjelang subuh dari hari yang tidak ditentukan. Dan seandainya hari yang dimaksud adalah hari yang ditentukan, niscaya ungkapannya tidak memakai harakat Tanwin karena termasuk Isim yang Ma'rifat dan dima'dul dari lafal As Sahar. Karena, bila dimaksud sebagai hari yang ditentukan pasti memakai Alif dan Lam. Apakah batu kerikil itu dihembuskan kepada keluarga Nabi Luth atau tidak? Sehubungan dengan hal ini ada dua pendapat. Menurut pendapat pertama yakni dihembuskan juga, berarti Istisna di sini bersifat Muttashil. Menurut pendapat kedua yakni, tidak dihembuskan berarti Istitsnanya bersifat Munqathi'. Akan tetapi pada akhirnya keluarga Nabi Luth diselamatkan dari azab itu

ar: نِّعۡمَةࣰ مِّنۡ عِندِنَاۚ كَذَ ٰلِكَ نَجۡزِی مَن شَكَرَ
ar_simple: نِعْمَةً مِنْ عِنْدِنَا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِي مَنْ شَكَرَ

t.sahih-int: As favor from us. Thus do We reward he who is grateful
t.asad: as a blessing from Us: thus do We reward all who are grateful
t.yusuf-ali: As a Grace from Us: thus do We reward those who give thanks
t.pickthall: As grace from Us. Thus We reward him who giveth thanks
t.arberry: a blessing from Us; even so We recompense him who is thankful
t.hilali-khan: As a Favour from Us, thus do We reward him who gives thanks (by obeying Us)
t.itani: A blessing from Us. Thus We reward the thankful.
t.mubarakpuri: As a favor from Us. Thus do We reward him who gives thanks.
t.qarib: as a blessing from us. Thus We reward those who are thankful
t.wahiduddin: as a blessing from Us: this is how We reward the thankful
t.shakir: A favor from Us; thus do We reward him who gives thanks
t.haleem: as a favour from Us: this is how We reward the thankful
t.maariful: as a grace from Us. This is how We reward the one who offers gratitude
t.khattab: as a blessing from Us. This is how We reward whoever gives thanks
t.ghali: A favor from Our Providence; thus We recompense him who thanks (Us)
t.irving: as a favor from Ourself; thus We reward anyone who acts grateful
t.iwq: As favor from us. Thus do We reward he who is grateful

t_id.kemenag: sebagai nikmat dari Kami. Demikianlah Kami memberi balasan kepada orang-orang yang bersyukur,
t_id.sabiq: Kami telah meniupkan angin keras yang melemparkan bebatuan kepada mereka, kecuali kaum Nabi Lûth yang beriman yang Kami selamatkan dari azab ini saat menjelang fajar, sebagai sebuah pemberian nikmat Kami untuk mereka. Dengan nikmat yang besar seperti itu, Kami memberikan balasan kepada orang yang mensyukuri nikmat Kami dengan cara beriman dan patuh.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (sebagai nikmat) menjadi mashdar, artinya sebagai pemberian nikmat. (dari sisi Kami. Demikianlah) sebagaimana pembalasan tersebut (Kami memberi balasan kepada orang-orang yang bersyukur) terhadap nikmat-nikmat Kami; dia adalah orang mukmin atau orang yang beriman kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya serta menaati keduanya.
t_id.iwq: sebagai nikmat dari Kami. Demikianlah Kami memberi balasan kepada orang-orang yang bersyukur,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «نعمة» مصدر، أي إنعاما «من عندنا كذلك» أي مثل ذلك الجزاء «نجزي من شكر» أنعمنا وهو مؤمن أو من آمن بالله ورسوله وأطاعهما
tafsir.siraj-ar: نِّعۡمَةٗ مِّنۡ عِندِنَاۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجۡزِي مَن شَكَرَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (sebagai nikmat) menjadi mashdar, artinya sebagai pemberian nikmat. (dari sisi Kami. Demikianlah) sebagaimana pembalasan tersebut (Kami memberi balasan kepada orang-orang yang bersyukur) terhadap nikmat-nikmat Kami; dia adalah orang mukmin atau orang yang beriman kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya serta menaati keduanya

ar: وَلَقَدۡ أَنذَرَهُم بَطۡشَتَنَا فَتَمَارَوۡا۟ بِٱلنُّذُرِ
ar_simple: وَلَقَدْ أَنْذَرَهُمْ بَطْشَتَنَا فَتَمَارَوْا بِالنُّذُرِ

t.sahih-int: And he had already warned them of Our assault, but they disputed the warning
t.asad: For he had truly warned them of Our punishing might; but they stubbornly cast doubt on these warnings
t.yusuf-ali: And (Lut) did warn them of Our Punishment, but they disputed about the Warning
t.pickthall: And he indeed had warned them of Our blow, but they did doubt the warnings
t.arberry: He had warned them of Our assault, but they disputed the warnings
t.hilali-khan: And he [Lout (Lot)] indeed had warned them of Our Grasp, but they did doubt the warnings
t.itani: He had warned them of Our onslaught, but they dismissed the warnings.
t.mubarakpuri: And he indeed had warned them of Our punishment, but they doubted the warnings!
t.qarib: Lot had already warned them of Our assault, but they dismissed the warning
t.wahiduddin: Lot warned them of Our punishment, but they disputed the warnings
t.shakir: And certainly he warned them of Our violent seizure, but they obstinately disputed the warning
t.haleem: He warned them of Our onslaught, but they dismissed the warning
t.maariful: And he (LūT) had certainly warned them of Our grasp, but they disputed the warnings
t.khattab: He had already warned them of Our ˹crushing˺ blow but they disputed the warnings
t.ghali: And indeed he had warned them of Our assault, yet they wrangled about the warnings
t.irving: He had warned them of Our onslaught, yet they discredited the warnings
t.iwq: And he had already warned them of Our assault, but they disputed the warning

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya dia (Luth) telah memperingatkan mereka akan azab-azab Kami, maka mereka mendustakan ancaman-ancaman itu.
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya Nabi Lûth telah mengingatkan kaumnya tentang azab Kami yang pedih. Tetapi mereka mengeluh--sebagai bentuk pendustaan--dengan peringatan-peringatan itu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya dia telah memperingatkan kepada mereka) yaitu Nabi Luth telah memberi peringatan kepada mereka (akan azab Kami) akan pembalasan Kami kepada mereka melalui azab-Nya (maka mereka mendustakan) mereka membantah dan mendustakan (ancaman-ancaman itu) ancaman-ancaman Nabi Luth.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya dia (Luth) telah memperingatkan mereka akan azab-azab Kami, maka mereka mendustakan ancaman-ancaman itu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد أنذرهم» خوفهم لوط «بطشتنا» أخذتنا إياهم بالعذاب «فتماروا» تجادلوا وكذبوا «بالنذر» بإنذاره
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ أَنذَرَهُم بَطۡشَتَنَا فَتَمَارَوۡاْ بِٱلنُّذُرِ (أَنذَرَهُم بَطْشَتَنَا: خَوَّفَهُمْ لُوطٌ - عليه السلام - بَاسَ اللهِ, فَتَمَارَوْا: شَكُّوا، وَكَذَّبُوا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya dia telah memperingatkan kepada mereka) yaitu Nabi Luth telah memberi peringatan kepada mereka (akan azab Kami) akan pembalasan Kami kepada mereka melalui azab-Nya (maka mereka mendustakan) mereka membantah dan mendustakan (ancaman-ancaman itu) ancaman-ancaman Nabi Luth

ar: وَلَقَدۡ رَ ٰوَدُوهُ عَن ضَیۡفِهِۦ فَطَمَسۡنَاۤ أَعۡیُنَهُمۡ فَذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابِی وَنُذُرِ
ar_simple: وَلَقَدْ رَاوَدُوهُ عَنْ ضَيْفِهِ فَطَمَسْنَا أَعْيُنَهُمْ فَذُوقُوا عَذَابِي وَنُذُرِ

t.sahih-int: And they had demanded from him his guests, but We obliterated their eyes, [saying], "Taste My punishment and warning
t.asad: and even demanded that he give up his guests [to them]: whereupon We deprived them of their sight [and thus told them, as it were]: “Taste, then, the suffering which I inflict when My warnings are disregarded!”
t.yusuf-ali: And they even sought to snatch away his guests from him, but We blinded their eyes. (They heard:) "Now taste ye My Wrath and My Warning
t.pickthall: They even asked of him his guests for an ill purpose. Then We blinded their eyes (and said): Taste now My punishment after My warnings
t.arberry: Even his guests they had solicited of him; so We obliterated their eyes, saying, 'Taste now My chastisement and My warnings
t.hilali-khan: And they indeed sought to shame his guest (by asking to commit sodomy with them). So We blinded their eyes, "Then taste you My Torment and My Warnings
t.itani: They even lusted for his guest, so We obliterated their eyes. “So taste My punishment and My warnings.”
t.mubarakpuri: And they indeed sought to shame his guests. So, We blinded their eyes (saying), "Then taste you My torment and My warnings."
t.qarib: They even lusted for his guest, so We blinded their eyes. "Taste My punishment and [the fulfillment of] My warnings
t.wahiduddin: they even wanted to seduce his guests -- but We blinded them, and said, "Taste My punishment now that you have scorned My warnings
t.shakir: And certainly they endeavored to turn him from his guests, but We blinded their eyes; so taste My chastisement and My warning
t.haleem: they even demanded his guests from him- so We sealed their eyes- ‘ Taste My [terrible] punishment and [the fulfilment of] My warnings!’
t.maariful: And they had even tried to tempt him against his guests (so that they may snatch them away for bad purpose,) but We blinded their eyes: “Now taste My torment and My warnings!”
t.khattab: And they even demanded his angel-guests from him, so We blinded their eyes. ˹And they were told,˺ “Taste then My punishment and warnings!”
t.ghali: And indeed they already solicited of him his guests, so We obliterated their eyes, (saying), "So taste My torment and My warnings
t.irving: They tried to coax his guests away from him, so We dazzled their eyes. "Taste My torment and My warnings
t.iwq: And they had demanded from him his guests, but We obliterated their eyes, saying, "Taste My punishment and warning

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya mereka telah membujuknya (agar menyerahkan) tamunya (kepada mereka), lalu Kami butakan mata mereka, maka rasakanlah azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku.
t_id.sabiq: Mereka membujuk Lûth supaya menyerahkan tamunya kepada mereka. Mata mereka kemudian Kami butakan sebagai balasan atas keinginan mereka itu. Dengan nada mengejek, dikatakan kepada mereka, "Rasakanlah azab dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya mereka telah membujuknya agar menyerahkan tamunya) mereka membujuknya supaya dia membiarkan mereka dengan orang-orang yang datang kepadanya sebagai tamu. Mereka bermaksud akan berbuat homosex dengan para tamunya itu, padahal para tamunya itu adalah malaikat-malaikat yang menjelma menjadi manusia (lalu Kami butakan mata mereka) Kami jadikan buta mata mereka dan Kami jadikan mata mereka tertutup rapat atau rata, sama rata dengan bagian muka yang lainnya. Yaitu, setelah malaikat Jibril menampar mereka dengan sayapnya (maka rasakanlah) maksudnya, Kami berkata kepada mereka, "Rasakanlah oleh kalian (azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku) ini". Yakni, ancaman dan peringatan-Ku ini; makna yang dimaksud adalah buah dan akibat daripada ancaman-Ku.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya mereka telah membujuknya (agar menyerahkan) tamunya (kepada mereka), lalu Kami butakan mata mereka, maka rasakanlah azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد راودوه عن ضيفه» أن يخلي بينهم وبين القوم الذين أتوه في صورة الأضياف ليخبثوا بهم وكانوا ملائكة «فطمسنا أعينهم» أعميناها وجعلناها بلا شق كباقي الوجه بأن صفقها جبريل بجناحه «فذوقوا» فقلنا لهم ذوقوا «عذابي ونذر» إنذاري وتخويفي، أي ثمرته وفائدته
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ رَٰوَدُوهُ عَن ضَيۡفِهِۦ فَطَمَسۡنَآ أَعۡيُنَهُمۡ فَذُوقُواْ عَذَابِي وَنُذُرِ (رَاوَدُوهُ: طَلَبُوا مِنْهُ أَنْ يَفْعَلُوا الفَاحِشَةَ بِهِمْ, فَطَمَسْنَا: أَعْمَيْنَا، وَحَجَبْنَا عَاقَبَهُمُ اللهُ بِالطَّمْسِ، ثُمَّ بِقَلْبِ قُرَاهُمْ، ثُمَّ أَمْطَرَهُمْ حِجَارَةً مِن طِينٍ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya mereka telah membujuknya agar menyerahkan tamunya) mereka membujuknya supaya dia membiarkan mereka dengan orang-orang yang datang kepadanya sebagai tamu. Mereka bermaksud akan berbuat homosex dengan para tamunya itu, padahal para tamunya itu adalah malaikat-malaikat yang menjelma menjadi manusia (lalu Kami butakan mata mereka) Kami jadikan buta mata mereka dan Kami jadikan mata mereka tertutup rapat atau rata, sama rata dengan bagian muka yang lainnya. Yaitu, setelah malaikat Jibril menampar mereka dengan sayapnya (maka rasakanlah) maksudnya, Kami berkata kepada mereka, "Rasakanlah oleh kalian (azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku) ini". Yakni, ancaman dan peringatan-Ku ini; makna yang dimaksud adalah buah dan akibat daripada ancaman-Ku

ar: وَلَقَدۡ صَبَّحَهُم بُكۡرَةً عَذَابࣱ مُّسۡتَقِرࣱّ
ar_simple: وَلَقَدْ صَبَّحَهُمْ بُكْرَةً عَذَابٌ مُسْتَقِرٌّ

t.sahih-int: And there came upon them by morning an abiding punishment
t.asad: And, indeed, abiding suffering did befall them early on the morrow
t.yusuf-ali: Early on the morrow an abiding Punishment seized them
t.pickthall: And in truth the punishment decreed befell them early in the morning
t.arberry: In the morning early there came upon them a settled chastisement
t.hilali-khan: And verily, an abiding torment seized them early in the morning
t.itani: Early morning brought upon them enduring punishment.
t.mubarakpuri: And verily, an abiding torment seized them early in the morning.
t.qarib: Early morning brought them enduring punishment
t.wahiduddin: and early in the morning the punishment decreed overtook them
t.shakir: And certainly a lasting chastisement overtook them in the morning
t.haleem: and early in the morning a punishment seized them that still remains
t.maariful: And on the next morning, a lasting torment overtook them
t.khattab: And indeed, by the early morning they were overwhelmed by an unrelenting torment
t.ghali: And indeed there already came upon them before sunrise a settled torment
t.irving: Early one, morning, unavoidable torment dawned upon them
t.iwq: And there came upon them by morning an abiding punishment

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya pada esok harinya mereka ditimpa azab yang kekal.
t_id.sabiq: Pagi-pagi sekali mereka telah dikejutkan oleh siksaan yang telah ditetapkan dan kekal. Dikatakan kepada mereka, "Rasakanlah azab dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku ini."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya pada esok harinya mereka ditimpa) yaitu, di waktu subuh dari hari yang tidak ditentukan itu (azab yang kekal) azab yang terus-menerus hingga sampai kepada saatnya azab akhirat.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya pada esok harinya mereka ditimpa azab yang kekal.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد صبحهم بكرة» وقت الصبح من يوم غير معين «عذاب مستقر» دائم متصل بعذاب الآخرة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ صَبَّحَهُم بُكۡرَةً عَذَابٞ مُّسۡتَقِرّٞ (بُكْرَةً: أَوَّلَ النَّهَارِ, مُّسْتَقِرٌّ: دَائِمٌ مُتَّصِلٌ بِعَذَابِ الآخِرَةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya pada esok harinya mereka ditimpa) yaitu, di waktu subuh dari hari yang tidak ditentukan itu (azab yang kekal) azab yang terus-menerus hingga sampai kepada saatnya azab akhirat

ar: فَذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابِی وَنُذُرِ
ar_simple: فَذُوقُوا عَذَابِي وَنُذُرِ

t.sahih-int: So taste My punishment and warning
t.asad: “Taste, then, the suffering which I inflict when My warnings are disregarded!”
t.yusuf-ali: So taste ye My Wrath and My Warning
t.pickthall: Now taste My punishment after My warnings
t.arberry: Taste now My chastisement and My warnings
t.hilali-khan: Then taste you My Torment and My Warnings
t.itani: So taste My punishment and My warnings.
t.mubarakpuri: Then taste you My torment and My warnings.
t.qarib: So taste My punishment [which is the fulfillment of] My warning
t.wahiduddin: Taste My punishment now that you have scorned My warnings
t.shakir: So taste My chastisement and My warning
t.haleem: ‘Taste My [terrible] punishment and [the fulfilment of] My warnings!’
t.maariful: “Now taste My torment and My warnings.”
t.khattab: ˹Again they were told,˺ “Taste now My punishment and warnings!”
t.ghali: So taste My torment and My warnings
t.irving: Taste My torment and My warnings
t.iwq: Thus taste My punishment and warning

t_id.kemenag: Maka rasakanlah azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku.
t_id.sabiq: Pagi-pagi sekali mereka telah dikejutkan oleh siksaan yang telah ditetapkan dan kekal. Dikatakan kepada mereka, "Rasakanlah azab dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku ini."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka rasakanlah azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku)
t_id.iwq: Maka rasakanlah azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فذوقوا عذابي ونذر»
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَذُوقُواْ عَذَابِي وَنُذُرِ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Maka rasakanlah azab-Ku dan ancaman-ancaman-Ku

ar: وَلَقَدۡ یَسَّرۡنَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ لِلذِّكۡرِ فَهَلۡ مِن مُّدَّكِرࣲ
ar_simple: وَلَقَدْ يَسَّرْنَا الْقُرْآنَ لِلذِّكْرِ فَهَلْ مِنْ مُدَّكِرٍ

t.sahih-int: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember
t.asad: Hence, indeed, We made this Qur’an easy to bear in mind: who, then, is willing to take it to heart
t.yusuf-ali: And We have indeed made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember: then is there any that will receive admonition
t.pickthall: And in truth We have made the Qur'an easy to remember; but is there any that remembereth
t.arberry: Now We have made the Koran easy for Remembrance. Is there any that will remember
t.hilali-khan: And indeed, We have made the Quran easy to understand and remember, then is there any that will remember (or receive admonition)
t.itani: We made the Quran easy to memorize. Is there anyone who would memorize?
t.mubarakpuri: And indeed, We have made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember; then is there any that will remember
t.qarib: Hence, We made it easy to bear this Qur'an in mind. Who, then, is willing to keep it in mind
t.wahiduddin: We have made it easy indeed to learn lessons from the Quran. Is there anyone who would receive admonition
t.shakir: And certainly We have made the Quran easy for remembrance, but is there anyone who will mind
t.haleem: We have made it easy to learn lessons from the Quran: will anyone take heed
t.maariful: Indeed We have made the Qur’ān easy for seeking advice. So, is there one to heed to the advice
t.khattab: And We have certainly made the Quran easy to remember. So is there anyone who will be mindful
t.ghali: And indeed We have already made the Qur'an easy for remembrance. Is there then any that will recollect
t.irving: We have made the Quran easy to memorize; yet will anyone memorize it
t.iwq: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami mudahkan Al Quran untuk pelajaran, maka adakah orang yang mengambil pelajaran?
t_id.sabiq: Kami telah memudahkan al-Qur'ân untuk dijadikan pelajaran dan nasihat. Maka adakah orang yang mau menjadikannya pelajaran?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami mudahkan Alquran untuk pelajaran, maka sudah adakah orang yang mengambil pelajaran?).
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami mudahkan Al Al-Quran untuk pelajaran, maka adakah orang yang mengambil pelajaran?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد يسرنا القرآن للذكر فهل مدكر»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ يَسَّرۡنَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ لِلذِّكۡرِ فَهَلۡ مِن مُّدَّكِرٖ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami mudahkan Alquran untuk pelajaran, maka sudah adakah orang yang mengambil pelajaran

ar: وَلَقَدۡ جَاۤءَ ءَالَ فِرۡعَوۡنَ ٱلنُّذُرُ
ar_simple: وَلَقَدْ جَاءَ آلَ فِرْعَوْنَ النُّذُرُ

t.sahih-int: And there certainly came to the people of Pharaoh warning
t.asad: Now surely, unto Pharaoh’s folk [too] came such warnings
t.yusuf-ali: To the People of Pharaoh, too, aforetime, came Warners (from Allah)
t.pickthall: And warnings came in truth unto the house of Pharaoh
t.arberry: The warnings came also to Pharaoh's folk
t.hilali-khan: And indeed, warnings came to the people of Fir'aun (Pharaoh) [through Musa (Moses) and Harun (Aaron)
t.itani: The warnings also came to the people of Pharaoh.
t.mubarakpuri: And indeed, warnings came to the people of Fir`awn.
t.qarib: The people of Pharaoh also received warnings
t.wahiduddin: Surely warners came to the people of Pharaoh
t.shakir: And certainly the warning came to Firon's people
t.haleem: The people of Pharaoh also received warnings
t.maariful: And the warners came to the family of Fir‘aun (Pharaoh)
t.khattab: And indeed, the warnings ˹also˺ came to the people of Pharaoh
t.ghali: And indeed the warnings already came (also) to the house of Firaawn; (Pharaoh)
t.irving: Warnings came to Pharaoh´s court
t.iwq: And there certainly came to the people of Pharaoh warning

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya telah datang kepada kaum Fir'aun ancaman-ancaman.
t_id.sabiq: Sungguh telah datang berbagai ancaman yang terus-menerus kepada kaum Fir'aun.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya telah datang kepada kaum Firaun) kepada Firaun dan kaumnya (ancaman-ancaman itu) ancaman-Ku melalui lisan Nabi Musa dan Nabi Harun, tetapi mereka masih tetap tidak mau beriman. Bahkan,
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya telah datang kepada kaum Fir'aun ancaman-ancaman.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد جاء آل فرعون» قومه معه «النذر» الإنذار على لسان موسى وهارون فلم يؤمنوا بل
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ جَآءَ ءَالَ فِرۡعَوۡنَ ٱلنُّذُرُ (آلَ فِرْعَوْنَ: أَتْبَاعَهُ، وَقَوْمَهُ, النُّذُرُ: الإِنْذَارُ بِالعُقُوبَةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya telah datang kepada kaum Firaun) kepada Firaun dan kaumnya (ancaman-ancaman itu) ancaman-Ku melalui lisan Nabi Musa dan Nabi Harun, tetapi mereka masih tetap tidak mau beriman. Bahkan

ar: كَذَّبُوا۟ بِءَایَـٰتِنَا كُلِّهَا فَأَخَذۡنَـٰهُمۡ أَخۡذَ عَزِیزࣲ مُّقۡتَدِرٍ
ar_simple: كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا كُلِّهَا فَأَخَذْنَاهُمْ أَخْذَ عَزِيزٍ مُقْتَدِرٍ

t.sahih-int: They denied Our signs, all of them, so We seized them with a seizure of one Exalted in Might and Perfect in Ability
t.asad: they, too, gave the lie to all Our messages: and thereupon We took them to task as only the Almighty, who determines all things, can take to task
t.yusuf-ali: The (people) rejected all Our Signs; but We seized them with such Penalty (as comes) from One Exalted in Power, able to carry out His Will
t.pickthall: Who denied Our revelations, every one. Therefore We grasped them with the grasp of the Mighty, the Powerful
t.arberry: They cried lies to Our signs, all of them, so We seized them with the seizing of One mighty, omnipotent
t.hilali-khan: (They) belied all Our Signs, so We seized them with a Seizure of the All-Mighty, All-Capable to carry out what he Will (Omnipotent)
t.itani: They rejected Our signs, all of them, so We seized them—the seizure of an Almighty Omnipotent.
t.mubarakpuri: (They) denied all Our signs, so We seized them with a punishment of the Almighty, All-Capable.
t.qarib: They rejected all Our signs, so We seized them as only the Almighty, the Dominant will
t.wahiduddin: They, too, rejected all Our signs. So We seized them with the seizure of One Mighty, Omnipotent
t.shakir: They rejected all Our communications, so We overtook them after the manner of a Mighty, Powerful One
t.haleem: They rejected all Our signs so We seized them with all Our might and power
t.maariful: (But) they rejected all Our signs; so We seized them- a seizure by One Mighty, Powerful
t.khattab: ˹But˺ they rejected all of Our signs, so We seized them with the ˹crushing˺ grip of the Almighty, Most Powerful
t.ghali: They cried lies to Our signs, all of them, so We took them (away) with the taking of an Ever-Mighty, Supreme Determiner
t.irving: Yet they rejected Our signs completely, so We seized them the way a Powerful, Competent person would seize [them]
t.iwq: And they denied Our signs, all of them, so We seized them with a seizure of one Exalted in Might and Perfect in Ability

t_id.kemenag: Mereka mendustakan mukjizat Kami semuanya, lalu Kami azab mereka sebagai azab dari Yang Maha Perkasa lagi Maha Kuasa
t_id.sabiq: Mereka telah mendustakan ayat-ayat Kami dan mukjizat yang dibawa oleh para rasul Kami. Mereka Kami hancurkan dengan cara Sang Mahaperkasa dan Mahakuasa.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (mereka mendustakan ayat-ayat Kami kesemuanya) yakni sembilan ayat yang diberikan kepada Nabi Musa (lalu Kami azab mereka) yakni, Kami turunkan azab kepada mereka (sebagai azab dari Yang Maha Perkasa) Yang Maha Kuat (lagi Maha Kuasa) tiada sesuatu pun yang dapat melemahkan dan menghalang-halangi-Nya.
t_id.iwq: Mereka mendustakan mukjizat Kami semuanya, lalu Kami azab mereka sebagai azab dari Yang Maha Perkasa lagi Maha Kuasa

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «كذبوا بآياتنا كلها» التسع التي أوتيها موسى «فأخذناهم» بالعذاب «أخذ عزيز» قوي «مقتدر» قادر لا يعجزه شيء
tafsir.siraj-ar: كَذَّبُواْ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَا كُلِّهَا فَأَخَذۡنَٰهُمۡ أَخۡذَ عَزِيزٖ مُّقۡتَدِرٍ (عَزِيزٍ: غَالِبٍ، قَوِيٍّ لَا يُغْلَبُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (mereka mendustakan ayat-ayat Kami kesemuanya) yakni sembilan ayat yang diberikan kepada Nabi Musa (lalu Kami azab mereka) yakni, Kami turunkan azab kepada mereka (sebagai azab dari Yang Maha Perkasa) Yang Maha Kuat (lagi Maha Kuasa) tiada sesuatu pun yang dapat melemahkan dan menghalang-halangi-Nya

ar: أَكُفَّارُكُمۡ خَیۡرࣱ مِّنۡ أُو۟لَـٰۤئِكُمۡ أَمۡ لَكُم بَرَاۤءَةࣱ فِی ٱلزُّبُرِ
ar_simple: أَكُفَّارُكُمْ خَيْرٌ مِنْ أُولَٰئِكُمْ أَمْ لَكُمْ بَرَاءَةٌ فِي الزُّبُرِ

t.sahih-int: Are your disbelievers better than those [former ones], or have you immunity in the scripture
t.asad: ARE, THEN, those of you who [now] deny the truth better than those others - or have you, perchance, [been promised] immunity in the [ancient] books of [divine] wisdom
t.yusuf-ali: Are your Unbelievers, (O Quraish), better than they? Or have ye an immunity in the Sacred Books
t.pickthall: Are your disbelievers better than those, or have ye some immunity in the scriptures
t.arberry: What, are your unbelievers better than those? Or have you an immunity in the Scrolls
t.hilali-khan: Are your disbelievers (O Quraish!) better than these [nations of Nuh (Noah), Lout (Lot), Salih (Saleh), and the people of Fir'aun (Pharaoh), etc., who were destroyed)? Or have you an immunity (against Our Torment) in the Divine Scriptures
t.itani: Are your unbelievers better than all those? Or do you have immunity in the scriptures?
t.mubarakpuri: Are your disbelievers better than these Or have you immunity in the Divine Scriptures
t.qarib: Are those among you who deny the truth any better than these others, or do you have immunity in the Books
t.wahiduddin: Are your people who deny the truth better than those? Or have you been given immunity in the Scriptures
t.shakir: Are the unbelievers of yours better than these, or is there an exemption for you in the scriptures
t.haleem: ‘Are your disbelievers any better than these? Were you given an exemption in the Scripture?’
t.maariful: Are the disbelievers among you in a better position than all of the aforesaid (punished people), or do you have immunity (recorded) in the sacred books
t.khattab: Now, are you ˹Meccan˺ disbelievers superior to those ˹destroyed peoples˺? Or have you ˹been granted˺ immunity ˹from punishment˺ in divine Books
t.ghali: Are your steadfast disbelievers more charitable (3) than those ones? Or even do you have an acquittal (from torment) in the Zubur? (i.e., better)
t.irving: Are your disbelievers any better than those men were, or have you some dispensation [to be found] in the Psalms
t.iwq: Are your disbelievers better than those former ones, or have you immunity in the scripture

t_id.kemenag: Apakah orang-orang kafirmu (hai kaum musyrikin) lebih baik dari mereka itu, atau apakah kamu telah mempunyai jaminan kebebasan (dari azab) dalam Kitab-kitab yang dahulu
t_id.sabiq: Apakah kalian, hai orang-orang kafir, lebih kuat dari kaum-kaum terdahulu yang telah dimusnahkan itu? Bahkan, apakah kalian mempunyai jaminan akan bebas dari siksaan Kami dalam kitab-kitab samawi yang telah diturunkan?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Apakah orang-orang kafir kalian) hai orang-orang Quraisy (lebih baik daripada mereka itu) yang telah disebutkan yaitu, mulai dari kaum Nabi Nuh hingga sampai kepada Firaun dan kaumnya yang tidak mendapatkan ampunan (atau apakah kalian telah mempunyai) hai orang-orang kafir Quraisy (jaminan kebebasan) dari azab (dalam kitab-kitab) yang terdahulu. Istifham yang terdapat pada dua tempat ini mengandung makna Nafi artinya, kenyataannya tidaklah demikian.
t_id.iwq: Apakah orang-orang kafirmu (hai kaum musyrikin) lebih baik dari mereka itu, atau apakah kamu telah mempunyai jaminan kebebasan (dari azab) dalam Kitab-kitab yang dahulu

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أكفاركم» يا قريش «خير من أولئكم» المذكورين من قوم نوح إلى فرعون فلم يعذروا «أم لكم» يا كفار قريش «براءة» من العذاب «في الزبر» الكتب والاستفهام في الموضعين بمعنى النفي أي ليس الأمر كذلك
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَكُفَّارُكُمۡ خَيۡرٞ مِّنۡ أُوْلَـٰٓئِكُمۡ أَمۡ لَكُم بَرَآءَةٞ فِي ٱلزُّبُرِ (الزُّبُرِ: الكُتُبِ المُنَزَّلَةِ عَلَى الأَنْبِيَاءِ عَلَيْهِمْ السَّلَام)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Apakah orang-orang kafir kalian) hai orang-orang Quraisy (lebih baik daripada mereka itu) yang telah disebutkan yaitu, mulai dari kaum Nabi Nuh hingga sampai kepada Firaun dan kaumnya yang tidak mendapatkan ampunan (atau apakah kalian telah mempunyai) hai orang-orang kafir Quraisy (jaminan kebebasan) dari azab (dalam kitab-kitab) yang terdahulu. Istifham yang terdapat pada dua tempat ini mengandung makna Nafi artinya, kenyataannya tidaklah demikian

ar: أَمۡ یَقُولُونَ نَحۡنُ جَمِیعࣱ مُّنتَصِرࣱ
ar_simple: أَمْ يَقُولُونَ نَحْنُ جَمِيعٌ مُنْتَصِرٌ

t.sahih-int: Or do they say, "We are an assembly supporting [each other]
t.asad: Or do they say, “We are a group united, [and therefore] bound to prevail”
t.yusuf-ali: Or do they say: "We acting together can defend ourselves
t.pickthall: Or say they: We are a host victorious
t.arberry: Or do they say, 'We are a congregation that shall be succoured
t.hilali-khan: Or do they say: "We are a great multitude, and we shall be victorious
t.itani: Or do they say, “We are united, and we will be victorious”?
t.mubarakpuri: Or say they: "We are a great multitude, victorious"
t.qarib: Or do they say, "We are united and bound to win
t.wahiduddin: Or do they say, "We are a united group, and we are bound to prevail
t.shakir: Or do they say: We are a host allied together to help each other
t.haleem: Do they perhaps say, ‘We are a great army and we shall be victorious’
t.maariful: Or do they say, “We are a large group, well-defended.”
t.khattab: Or do they say, “We are all ˹a˺ united ˹front˺, bound to prevail.”
t.ghali: Or do they say, "We, all together, will vindicate ourselves
t.irving: Or do they say: "We all support one another
t.iwq: Or do they say, "We are an assembly supporting each other

t_id.kemenag: Atau apakah mereka mengatakan: "Kami adalah satu golongan yang bersatu yang pasti menang".
t_id.sabiq: Atau apakah orang-orang kafir itu mengatakan, "Kami adalah golongan yang bersatu yang pasti menang dan tidak terkalahkan oleh musuh?"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Atau apakah mereka mengatakan) yakni orang-orang kafir Quraisy, ("Kami adalah suatu golongan yang bersatu) orang-orang yang bersatu dalam satu golongan (yang pasti menang") atas Muhammad. Sewaktu Abu Jahal mengatakan dalam perang Badar, bahwa sesungguhnya kami adalah golongan yang bersatu yang pasti menang. Maka turunlah firman-Nya,
t_id.iwq: Atau apakah mereka mengatakan: "Kami adalah satu golongan yang bersatu yang pasti menang".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أم يقولون» أي كفار قريش «نحن جميع» جمع «منتصر» على محمد، ولما قال أبو جهل يوم بدر إنا جمع منتصر نزل
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَمۡ يَقُولُونَ نَحۡنُ جَمِيعٞ مُّنتَصِرٞ (نَحْنُ جَمِيعٌ مُّنتَصِرٌ: جَمَاعَةٌ مُنْتَصِرَةٌ لَا يَغْلِبُنَا مَنْ أَرَادَنَا بِسُوءٍ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Atau apakah mereka mengatakan) yakni orang-orang kafir Quraisy, ("Kami adalah suatu golongan yang bersatu) orang-orang yang bersatu dalam satu golongan (yang pasti menang") atas Muhammad. Sewaktu Abu Jahal mengatakan dalam perang Badar, bahwa sesungguhnya kami adalah golongan yang bersatu yang pasti menang. Maka turunlah firman-Nya

ar: سَیُهۡزَمُ ٱلۡجَمۡعُ وَیُوَلُّونَ ٱلدُّبُرَ
ar_simple: سَيُهْزَمُ الْجَمْعُ وَيُوَلُّونَ الدُّبُرَ

t.sahih-int: [Their] assembly will be defeated, and they will turn their backs [in retreat]
t.asad: [Yet] the hosts [of those who deny the truth] shall be routed, and they shall turn their backs [in flight]
t.yusuf-ali: Soon will their multitude be put to flight, and they will show their backs
t.pickthall: The hosts will all be routed and will turn and flee
t.arberry: Certainly the host shall be routed, and turn their backs
t.hilali-khan: Their multitude will be put to flight, and they will show their backs
t.itani: The multitude will be defeated, and they will turn their backs.
t.mubarakpuri: Their multitude will be put to flight, and they will show their backs.
t.qarib: They will all be defeated, and they will turn their backs and flee
t.wahiduddin: The hosts shall soon be routed and they shall be put to flight
t.shakir: Soon shall the hosts be routed, and they shall turn (their) backs
t.haleem: Their forces will be routed and they will turn tail and flee
t.maariful: Soon this ‘large group’ shall be defeated, and all of them will turn their backs
t.khattab: ˹Soon˺ their united front will be defeated and ˹forced to˺ flee
t.ghali: Soon the gathering will be routed and (they) will turn their backs
t.irving: They will all be defeated together and turn their backs [to run away]
t.iwq: Their assembly will be defeated, and they will turn their backs in retreat

t_id.kemenag: Golongan itu pasti akan dikalahkan dan mereka akan mundur ke belakang.
t_id.sabiq: Golongan ini pasti akan terkalahkan. Mereka akan lari mundur ke belakang.
t_id.jalalayn-id: ("Golongan Itu pasti akan dikalahkan dan mereka akan mundur ke belakang") akhirnya mereka dikalahkan oleh Nabi Muhammad saw. dalam perang Badar, dan Nabi Muhammad saw. mendapat kemenangan yang gemilang atas mereka.
t_id.iwq: Golongan itu pasti akan dikalahkan dan mereka akan mundur ke belakang.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «سيهزم الجمع ويولون الدبر» فهزموا ببدر ونصر رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم عليهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: سَيُهۡزَمُ ٱلۡجَمۡعُ وَيُوَلُّونَ ٱلدُّبُرَ (الْجَمْعُ: جَمَاعَةُ كُفَّارِ مَكَّةَ, وَيُوَلُّونَ الدُّبُرَ: يَفِرُّونَ مُنْهَزِمِينَ، قَدْ وَلَّوْكُمْ أَدْبَارَهُمْ؛ وَقَدْ حَصَلَ هَذَا فِي غَزْوَةِ بَدْرٍ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Golongan Itu pasti akan dikalahkan dan mereka akan mundur ke belakang") akhirnya mereka dikalahkan oleh Nabi Muhammad saw. dalam perang Badar, dan Nabi Muhammad saw. mendapat kemenangan yang gemilang atas mereka

ar: بَلِ ٱلسَّاعَةُ مَوۡعِدُهُمۡ وَٱلسَّاعَةُ أَدۡهَىٰ وَأَمَرُّ
ar_simple: بَلِ السَّاعَةُ مَوْعِدُهُمْ وَالسَّاعَةُ أَدْهَىٰ وَأَمَرُّ

t.sahih-int: But the Hour is their appointment [for due punishment], and the Hour is more disastrous and more bitter
t.asad: But nay - the Last Hour is the time when they shall truly meet their fate; and that Last Hour will be most calamitous, and most bitter
t.yusuf-ali: Nay, the Hour (of Judgment) is the time promised them (for their full recompense): And that Hour will be most grievous and most bitter
t.pickthall: Nay, but the Hour (of doom) is their appointed tryst, and the Hour will be more wretched and more bitter (than their earthly failure)
t.arberry: Nay, but the Hour is their tryst, and the Hour is very calamitous and bitter
t.hilali-khan: Nay, but the Hour is their appointed time (for their full recompense), and the Hour will be more grievous and more bitter
t.itani: The Hour is their appointed time—the Hour is more disastrous, and most bitter.
t.mubarakpuri: Nay, but the Hour is their appointed time, and the Hour will be more grievous and more bitter.
t.qarib: Rather the Hour is their appointed time and the Hour is more disastrous and bitter
t.wahiduddin: Indeed, the Hour of Doom is their appointed time, and the Last Hour will be the most severe, and the most bitter
t.shakir: Nay, the hour is their promised time, and the hour shall be most grievous and bitter
t.haleem: But the Hour is their appointed time- the Hour is more severe and bitter
t.maariful: Rather the Hour (of judgment) is their appointed time (for their full recompense), and the Hour is more calamitous and much more bitter
